rules: Prefer functional components with hooks over class components.
Keep components small and focused; extract reusable pieces into separate components.
Avoid deeply nested prop-drilling; use context or custom hooks when data is shared widely.
Use `useState`, `useReducer`, and custom hooks for local and domain logic.
Derive state when possible instead of duplicating it (e.g., compute counts from the todos array).
Keep side effects in `useEffect` / custom hooks; avoid side effects in render.
Define explicit types or interfaces for core domain objects (e.g., `Todo`).
Prefer `type` aliases for simple shapes and union types.
Avoid using `any`; use unknown, generics, or more specific types.
Use Tailwind utility classes directly in JSX for layout and spacing.
Group utility classes in a consistent order (layout → spacing → typography → colors → effects).
Extract small presentational components (e.g., `Button`, `Card`) when class lists grow too long.
Prefer semantic HTML elements (button, form, input, ul/li) styled with Tailwind rather than generic `div`s.
Ensure interactive elements are real buttons/links, not clickable `div`s.
Provide visible focus styles; do not remove them without replacing with custom focus states.
Support keyboard interactions (Enter/Space on buttons, proper labels for inputs).
Colocate components, styles, and tests where it makes sense (e.g., same folder).
Use clear naming: `*Page` for route-level components, `*Item` or `*Card` for list entries.
Keep `App.tsx` thin; push domain logic into hooks/components.
Prefer early returns to reduce nesting.
Avoid commented-out code in committed files.
Keep files focused; if a file grows too large or mixed-responsibility, split it.
Use React hooks and optional context for state management.
Core todo state lives in either a custom hook or a context.
Always treat state as immutable and persist to localStorage after each change.
Use Tailwind utility classes directly on JSX elements for layout and spacing.
Extract small reusable components instead of creating large custom CSS files.
Keep class lists readable by grouping related utilities.
For a simple single-page Todo app, React Router is not strictly required.
warnings: Not all files must exist on day one; this layout is the target design.
Tailwind is configured via tailwind.config and imported in src/styles/index.css.
Use Vitest or Jest for unit tests, colocated next to components.

rules: React component structure and state management
Tailwind CSS usage and styling conventions
Accessibility, UX, and general code quality guidelines
